Title: Identification of a central regulator of ginkgolide biosynthesis in Ginkgo biloba that integrates jasmonate and light signaling
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ginkgolides (including bilobalide, ginkgolides A, B, C)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses GbEAG transcriptional activity and thereby negatively regulates ginkgolide biosynthesis; MeJA alleviates this repression.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate (MeJA); light/dark context discussed for pathway output
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbJAZ3 directly interacts with GbEAG in yeast, in vitro, and in planta. 2. GbJAZ3 suppresses GbEAG-mediated activation of GbIDI and GbCYP7005C3 promoters. 3. MeJA alleviates GbJAZ3 repression, enabling JA-induced ginkgolide biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; pull-down assay; split-LUC complementation; dual-luciferase repression/relief assay with MeJA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal diterpene lactones with potential to prevent and treat cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion states JA likely triggers 26S proteasome-mediated breakdown of JAZ proteins, but direct degradation of GbJAZ3 was not experimentally shown in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ginkgolides (including bilobalide, ginkgolides A, B, C)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ginkgolide biosynthesis / isoprenoid precursor supply via MEP and MVA pathways
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Interconverts IPP and DMAPP to balance the isoprenoid precursor pool for terpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key biosynthetic gene; controls metabolic flow in MEP and MVA pathways
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed in roots; induced by MeJA, peaking around 3 h
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 400 μM MeJA treatment; light/dark in coordinated regulation experiments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive pathway gene whose expression is activated by GbEAG and associated with enhanced ginkgolide accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbIDI is highly expressed in roots and induced by MeJA. 2. GbEAG directly binds the GCC-box in the GbIDI promoter and activates transcription. 3. GbIDI expression increases in GbEAG-overexpressing roots and decreases in GbEAG-silenced roots, paralleling ginkgolide levels.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase; expression analysis in GbEAG overexpression and silencing lines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ginkgolides are medicinal terpenoids used against cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes discrepancy between two genome versions for GbIDI promoter/coding region; authors cloned promoter PQ356346 containing GCC-box and coding sequence PQ356344 used for validation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ginkgolides (including bilobalide, ginkgolides A, B, C)
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream / early committed skeletal modification step after levopimaradiene formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cytochrome P450-mediated skeletal modification reaction in ginkgolide biosynthesis; identified as the first skeletal modification CYP450 enzyme in the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms essential downstream intermediate(s) leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key biosynthetic gene; first skeletal modification CYP450 step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed in roots; induced by MeJA, peaking around 3 h
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 400 μM MeJA treatment; light/dark in coordinated regulation experiments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive pathway gene activated by GbEAG; its higher expression associates with increased ginkgolide accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbCYP7005C3 is highly expressed in roots and induced by MeJA. 2. GbEAG directly binds the GCC-box in the GbCYP7005C3 promoter and strongly activates transcription. 3. GbCYP7005C3 expression rises in GbEAG-overexpressing roots and drops in GbEAG-silenced roots, in parallel with ginkgolide accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase; expression analysis in GbEAG overexpression and silencing lines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ginkgolides are medicinal terpenoids used against cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter accession PQ356347. Discussion states GbCYP7005C3 is the first skeletal modification CYP450 enzyme in the ginkgolide pathway.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ginkgolides (including bilobalide, ginkgolides A, B, C)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Central positive regulator that promotes ginkgolide biosynthesis and accumulation in roots.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF family, ERF-B3 subclass; AP2 DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via direct binding to GCC-box elements in target promoters; activity repressed by JAZ protein in JA-dependent manner
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates GbIDI and GbCYP7005C3 transcription; positively associated with increased expression of GbGGPPS1 and GbLPS in transgenic roots
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: methyl jasmonate/jasmonic acid; light via GbHY5; repression by GbJAZ3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Identified by the paper as a critical/central regulator and hub integrating JA and light signaling for ginkgolide biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No stable complex established; functions through direct interaction with GbJAZ3 repressor
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots; strongly induced by MeJA; expression enhanced under light plus MeJA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 400 μM MeJA; light versus dark; transient 3 h induction stronger than 6 h
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: No direct feedback loop established; acts as integration node receiving JA and light inputs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coexpression analysis; qRT-PCR; transgenic root overexpression; VIGS knockdown; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase; subcellular localization; transcriptional activation assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbEAG was identified from transcriptome/coexpression analyses as the AP2/ERF TF most tightly associated with ginkgolide biosynthetic genes. 2. GbEAG directly binds GCC-boxes in GbIDI and GbCYP7005C3 promoters and activates their transcription. 3. Overexpression of GbEAG increases root ginkgolide contents, whereas silencing decreases them. 4. GbEAG interacts with GbJAZ3, which represses its activity in a JA-dependent manner. 5. GbHY5 activates GbEAG transcription under light conditions, placing GbEAG at the convergence of JA and light signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal diterpene lactones with potential to prevent and treat cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target for increasing ginkgolide content in ginkgo; potential use in allele mining, promoter editing/bashing, and transgenic or gene-editing strategies to create ginkgolide-rich varieties, with possible benefit from combining favorable light/JA-responsive regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression in transgenic roots and transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: N-terminal region contains transcriptional activation domain. Named ETHYLENE RESPONSE FACTOR ASSOCIATED WITH GINKGOLIDE BIOSYNTHESIS.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ginkgolides (including bilobalide, ginkgolides A, B, C)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive upstream light-response regulator that enhances ginkgolide biosynthesis by activating GbEAG expression.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via binding to G-box in GbEAG promoter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GbEAG transcription; indirectly promotes expression of multiple ginkgolide biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: light; MeJA responsiveness enhanced under light conditions
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central light-response regulator mediating light induction of ginkgolide biosynthesis upstream of GbEAG.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in young stems and leaves, followed by fibrous roots; induced more strongly by MeJA under light than dark
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light versus dark; 400 μM MeJA after full-light or full-dark treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression and knockdown in ginkgo roots; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase; Y1H; EMSA; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Light promotes ginkgolide accumulation, and GbHY5 expression correlates with this response. 2. GbHY5 overexpression increases, and silencing decreases, ginkgolide accumulation in roots. 3. GbHY5 positively regulates GbEAG expression. 4. GbHY5 directly binds G-box 2 in the GbEAG promoter and activates its transcription, linking light input to the GbEAG-centered regulatory module.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal diterpene lactones with potential to prevent and treat cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream engineering target to enhance light-responsive ginkgolide production, potentially in combination with GbEAG-centered strategies.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression and transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion suggests possible shoot-to-root movement based on HY5 literature, but this was not directly tested here.
